questions.question: What is a political party?
questions.answer: A political party is a group of people who come together to contest elections and hold power in government.

questions.question: Name the three components of a political party.
questions.answer: Leaders, active members, and followers.

questions.question: What is the main aim of political parties?
questions.answer: The main aim is to gain political power and form government.

questions.question: Which body registers political parties in India?
questions.answer: Election Commission of India.

questions.question: What is a multiparty system?
questions.answer: A system in which many political parties compete in elections.

questions.question: What is a coalition government?
questions.answer: A government formed by an alliance of several political parties.

questions.question: Name one country with a one-party system.
questions.answer: China.

questions.question: Name one country with a two-party system.
questions.answer: United States of America.

questions.question: Which system of political parties is followed in India?
questions.answer: Multiparty system.

questions.question: What role do political parties play in elections?
questions.answer: They select candidates and contest elections.

questions.question: What role do political parties play in government formation?
questions.answer: The party that wins the majority of seats forms the government.

questions.question: What is the role of opposition parties?
questions.answer: Opposition parties criticize government policies and suggest alternatives.

questions.question: What are national political parties?
questions.answer: National parties operate across several states and influence national politics.

questions.question: What are state political parties?
questions.answer: State parties operate mainly in a particular state and focus on regional issues.

questions.question: Name one national party in India.
questions.answer: Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP).

questions.question: Name one state party in India.
questions.answer: Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK).

questions.question: What is meant by internal democracy in political parties?
questions.answer: Internal democracy means that party members participate in decision-making.

questions.question: What is dynastic succession in politics?
questions.answer: Dynastic succession means leadership passing within a family.

questions.question: What is money power in politics?
questions.answer: Money power refers to the use of money to influence elections and political decisions.

questions.question: What is the anti-defection law?
questions.answer: The anti-defection law prevents elected representatives from changing parties after elections.

questions.question: Why are political reforms needed?
questions.answer: Political reforms are needed to improve transparency and accountability in political parties.

questions.question: How do political parties shape public opinion?
questions.answer: They raise issues and conduct campaigns to influence people’s views.

questions.question: How do political parties help citizens?
questions.answer: They help citizens communicate with the government and raise public issues.

questions.question: Why are regional parties important?
questions.answer: Regional parties represent the interests of specific regions.

questions.question: Why are political parties important for democracy?
questions.answer: They organize elections, represent people, and help form governments.
